head.hi: President Roosevelt to the Acting Secretary of State
head.note: Repeated to the Chairman of the American delegation as telegram No. 103, July 4, 11 p.m.

opener.dateline:
U. S. S. “Indianapolis,” July 4, 1933—3 p.m.
[Received 5:18 p.m.]

div.p: 24. For Hull and Delegation. Will cable you more at length about tariffs tomorrow but as a preliminary I send you the following thoughts.

list.item: 1st. I think you can make definite offer to any nation to discuss with us reciprocal tariff agreementsFor correspondence relating to reciprocal trade agreement policy, see pp. 921 ff; for bilateral negotiations with individual countries, see vol. ii under Canada, New Zealand, Norway, Portugal, and Sweden, vol. ii under Argentina, and vol. v under Brazil and Colombia. in Washington at their convenience.

2d. Would it be possible to work out a formula for an extension of tariff truce and for tariff adjustments downward basing this on (a) the only changes in existing schedules could be made by a
surcharge or rebate that would be automatically added or deducted to offset fluctuations of exchange during period in which we are working toward more stable conditions; (b) the standard tariff on which this surcharge or rebate is based should be founded on thought that as to manufactured goods it should not in any case be a complete embargo but should be low enough to allow some goods to enter even if the percentage these bear to a nation’s own production is as low as 2 or 3 percent; (c) as to agricultural and raw metal products I hope we can press for international production limitations on as many products as possible even if such agreements are made on experimental oasis for a limited period.

div.p: I think it vital for our whole delegation to stress the many things of world importance which the Conference can accomplish and that a defeatist attitude just because temporary gold stabilization is overboard is a serious responsibility for any nation to assume. I hope Great Britain and most of the smaller nations will go along with us in our sincere efforts.
